mnemonics: ISA: Instruction Set Architecture - The 'Contract' between hardware and software.
RISC: Reduced Instruction Set Computer - Fewer, simpler instructions for speed.
keyPoints: The Instruction Cycle (Fetch, Decode, Execute) is the fundamental sequence all CPUs follow.
The Program Counter (PC) holds the address of the next instruction to be fetched.
An Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) defines the interface between software and hardware.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the Program Counter (holds address) and the Instruction Register (holds the actual instruction code).
Assuming all instructions take the same number of clock cycles to execute (e.g., a multiply takes longer than an add).
